name: Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop
name_original: Camino del Cielo – vuelta a los Mallos de Riglos trail running
description: This difficult 3.2-mile trail running route offers spectacular views of the Mallos de Riglos and the Gállego River in the Spanish Pre-Pyrene
region_intro: Jogging routes around Las Peñas De Riglos are set within the dramatic landscapes of the Spanish Pre-Pyrenees, characterized by the colossal, reddish conglomerate rock formations known as the Mallos de Riglos. These vertical pillars, sculpted by erosion, provide a unique backdrop for running. The region also features trails along the Gállego River and diverse Pre-Pyrenean scenery, including parched terrains and varied flora. This area offers a range of running experiences amidst its distinctive natural features. Best jogging routes around Las Peñas De Riglos The most popular jogging route is Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes about 55 minutes to complete. This circular route encircles the Mallos de Riglos, offering views of the rock formations. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Colorado – Mallos de Riglos loop from Riglos, a difficult 17.9 miles (28.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and expansive views of the Mallos and surrounding landscape. Local runners also love the View of Mallos de Riglos – Mirador del Colorado loop from Riglos, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through the Pre-Pyrenean terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Jogging around Las Peñas De Riglos is defined by its towering conglomerate rock formations, the Gállego River, and the presence of Eurasian griffon vulture colonies. The network offers options for various ability levels, from shorter loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Las Peñas De Riglos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Las Peñas De Riglos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: There are over 50 running routes around Las Peñas de Riglos, offering a wide range of experiences amidst the dramatic Pre-Pyrenean landscape. These routes cater to various fitness levels, though many are considered challenging.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: The terrain is primarily characterized by the colossal, reddish conglomerate rock formations known as the Mallos de Riglos. You'll also find trails along the Gállego River and through diverse Pre-Pyrenean scenery, including parched terrains and varied flora. Expect significant elevation changes on many routes.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: While many routes in Las Peñas de Riglos are rated as difficult, there are options that can be adapted for those with good fitness. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950292551/mallos-de-riglos-path-of-heaven-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, 3.2-mile circular route that offers spectacular views, though it still involves significant elevation gain. It's recommended to assess your fitness level carefully.

Q: What makes the running experience in Las Peñas de Riglos unique?
A: The unique experience comes from running amidst the awe-inspiring Mallos de Riglos, towering up to 300 meters. The area is also home to one of Europe's largest colonies of Eurasian griffon vultures, offering opportunities for wildlife spotting as you run. The combination of dramatic rock formations, river views, and diverse Pre-Pyrenean flora creates a truly memorable backdrop.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: The running routes in Las Peñas de Riglos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the spectacular views of the Mallos, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the unique natural environment.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950292551/mallos-de-riglos-path-of-heaven-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which encircles the Mallos and provides stunning vistas. Other longer loops like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31847209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mallos de Riglos – Mirador del Colorado loop from Riglos&lt;/a&gt; also offer a complete circuit experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like griffon vultures and other local fauna. Be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: While many routes are challenging, families with older children who are accustomed to hiking can enjoy some of the shorter, less strenuous paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950292551/mallos-de-riglos-path-of-heaven-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos: Path of Heaven Loop&lt;/a&gt;, though it has elevation, is a manageable distance for active families looking for scenic views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while running?
A: The most prominent landmarks are the Mallos de Riglos themselves, with individual formations like "El Puro" and "Pisón." You can also spot Eurasian griffon vultures soaring overhead. Key viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7094510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Viewpoint (Mirador de los Buitres)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4630341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colorado Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive panoramas of the rock formations and surrounding landscape.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around the village of Riglos, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. Specific parking areas may be designated for trail access, so it's good to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: The region is suitable for outdoor activities for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for running. Summers can be hot, especially in the parched Pre-Pyrenean terrains, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended during these months. Winters are cooler but can still be good for running, though some higher trails might be affected by weather.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners seeking a significant challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10465517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Colorado – Mallos de Riglos loop from Riglos&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 17.9-mile path with substantial elevation gain, providing an intense workout and expansive views of the Mallos and surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I observe wildlife while jogging in Las Peñas de Riglos?
A: Yes, wildlife observation is a significant part of the experience. The Mallos are home to one of Europe's largest colonies of Eurasian griffon vultures, which you can frequently see soaring above. The ARCAZ Interpretation Center in Riglos offers more information on these birds of prey. You might also encounter other local fauna typical of the Pre-Pyrenean environment.
tips: Nestled in one of the most spectacular corners of the Hoya de Huesca, the town of Riglos stands out in all possible aspects for its beauty. Going into its streets is to fall asleep before the magnitude of the rocks that shelter it.
The last (or the first, depending on the direction of travel) of the splendid viewpoints on the route.
Right at the beginning it goes up well, but you will be rewarded with good views.

faqs.question: Is the Camino del Cielo – return to the Mallos de Riglos trail running suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While generally described as moderate, this trail running route includes sections with rocky switchbacks, loose stones, and steep inclines/descents. Good fitness and careful footing are recommended, especially for those new to trail running.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and engaging. You'll encounter reasonably rocky switchback climbs and descents. Some sections feature cobbled and steep areas with loose stones, so careful footing is essential.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is popular and starts directly from the village of Riglos. Parking is typically available within or near the village, providing convenient access to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The Spanish Pre-Pyrenees region generally offers pleasant conditions for trail running outside of the hottest summer months or potential winter snow. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and clear views of the Mallos de Riglos.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking views of the imposing, rust-red conglomerate rock formations known as the Mallos de Riglos, including individual pinnacles like Mallo Pisón and El Puro. You'll also get panoramic views of the Gállego River, the surrounding countryside, and glimpses of the Pyrenees. Don't miss the Colorado Viewpoint and the general View of Mallos de Riglos. The area is also home to one of Europe's largest colonies of Eurasian griffon vultures, which you can frequently spot soaring overhead.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with several other trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse, Circular de Riglos, Camino Natural de la Hoya de Huesca, and Sendero Histórico: Huesca.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs within the Monumento Natural de los Mallos de Riglos should be checked. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Camino del Cielo is located within the Monumento Natural de los Mallos de Riglos. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for accessing the trails themselves. However, always respect local regulations and signage.

faqs.question: What is the strategic advantage of running the trail clockwise or counter-clockwise?
faqs.answer: If you tackle the route clockwise, you'll experience a short, steep ascent followed by a longer, more moderate descent. Conversely, going counter-clockwise offers a more prolonged but progressively inclined ascent, which some runners might prefer for a steady climb.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The most prominent wildlife you're likely to encounter are the Eurasian griffon vultures. The Mallos de Riglos host one of Europe's largest colonies, so you'll frequently see these majestic birds soaring high above the rock formations.

faqs.question: Is the trail well-marked?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally well-signposted, making navigation straightforward and allowing you to focus on the stunning scenery.
